text: Adsorption in which forces are similar to van der Waals forces is called:
explanation: Physisorption (physical adsorption): weak van der Waals forces, low \(\Delta H\), reversible, multilayer possible.
name: Physisorption

text: Freundlich adsorption isotherm is given by:
explanation: Freundlich isotherm: \( \frac{x}{m} = kP^{1/n} \) or \( \log\frac{x}{m} = \log k + \frac{1}{n}\log P \).
name: Freundlich Isotherm

explanation: Tyndall effect: scattering of light by colloidal particles, making the path of light visible. True solutions don't show it.
name: Tyndall Effect

text: The phenomenon of movement of colloidal particles under electric field is called:
explanation: Electrophoresis: movement of charged colloidal particles in an electric field. Electro-osmosis: movement of dispersion medium.
name: Electrophoresis

text: Gold number is a measure of:
explanation: Gold number: minimum mass (in mg) of protective colloid to prevent coagulation of 10 mL gold sol by 1 mL 10% NaCl. Lower gold number = better protection.
name: Gold Number

text: Coagulation of a colloid can be brought about by:
explanation: Coagulation occurs by adding electrolyte with oppositely charged ions, which neutralise the charge on colloidal particles.
name: Coagulation

text: Hardy-Schulze rule states that coagulating power of an ion is:
explanation: Hardy-Schulze Rule: higher the valency of the coagulating ion, greater its coagulating power. Al³⁺ > Ca²⁺ > Na⁺ for negative sol.
name: Hardy-Schulze Rule

explanation: Emulsion: liquid dispersed in another liquid. Types: O/W (oil in water, e.g., milk) and W/O (water in oil, e.g., butter).
name: Emulsion Definition

text: The size of colloidal particles ranges from:
explanation: Colloidal particles: 1–1000 nm (1–100 nm in most definitions). True solution: < 1 nm; suspension: > 1000 nm.
name: Colloidal Particle Size

options: Lower activation energy and lower \(\Delta H\)
Higher activation energy and higher \(\Delta H\)
explanation: Chemisorption: chemical bond formation, high \(E_a\), high \(\Delta H_{ads}\) (40-400 kJ/mol), monolayer, irreversible.
name: Chemisorption vs Physisorption

text: Brownian motion of colloidal particles is due to:
explanation: Brownian motion: random zigzag motion of colloidal particles caused by unequal bombardment by surrounding solvent molecules.
name: Brownian Motion

options: Converting precipitate back into colloidal form
explanation: Peptization: converting a freshly prepared precipitate into colloidal form by adding a peptizing agent (common ion).
name: Peptization

options: Remove crystalloids from colloidal solution using semi-permeable membrane
explanation: Dialysis: purification of colloids by passing small ions/molecules through a semi-permeable membrane while retaining colloidal particles.
name: Dialysis

options: Decrease in entropy of the system
explanation: In adsorption, molecules become more ordered on surface → decrease in entropy (\(\Delta S < 0\)). Yet process is spontaneous because \(\Delta H < 0\) (exothermic).
name: Entropy in Adsorption
